name: counting-house
summary: A merchant's counting house or private bank. High counter separating clerks from clients, ledgers and scales, a heavy vault door in the back, guards at the entrance. Quiet, formal, precise.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: rectangle with counter dividing public and private zones

accent.purpose: polished private zone behind the counter

primary_palette.note: classic counting-house — long clerk counter, desks behind
bank — vault visible behind the counter
partnership house — open desks for senior partners

story_prompts: noble deposit — a baron with chests of gold, clerks counting, guards watching
audit — a guild inspector going line-by-line, clerks sweating
robbery — vault cracked overnight, owner pale, magistrate taking notes
after hours — one senior partner alone with a ledger, hiding a discrepancy

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: steady clean light (0.5) — clerks need to read columns of numbers

anti_patterns: the counter is a hard barrier — clients one side, clerks the other, never ambiguous
vault on an interior wall against stone — never on an exterior wall
clerks face clients — desks behind them perpendicular so they can turn quickly
